data.title: Meihua International Medical Technologies receives Nasdaq notice
data.description:
• Meihua International Medical Technologies (MHUA) announced  on Friday that it had received a letter from the Nasdaq on December 2, 2025, notifying it that the Nasdaq staff had determined to delist the company's ordinary shares because the company failed to regain compliance with the Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).
  • Additionally, on December 1, 2025, the Nasdaq staff notified the company that it no longer complies with Listing Rule 5550(a)(4), which requires Nasdaq-listed companies to have at least 500,000 publicly held shares. 
  • The trading of the company’s ordinary shares will be suspended at the opening of business on December 9, 2025.
  • The company submitted the hearing request on December 4, 2025.

data.title: Parsons secures spot on $1B sound transit design contract
data.description:
• Parsons (PSN) on Friday said it has been selected for Sound Transit’s 2025 $1 billion ceiling Multiple Award Task Order Contract (MATOC) for design services.
  • This five-year contract, with two potential one-year extensions, will support Sound Transit’s $60 billion capital program.
  • Under this MATOC, Parsons will provide architecture, engineering, and related services for projects, including light rail extensions, system resiliency and sustainability improvements, and state of good repair initiatives.
  • PSN +1.94% after hours to $68.0.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ServiceNow announces 5-for-1 stock split
data.description:
• ServiceNow (NOW) shareholders approved a 5-for-1 split of the company’s common stock.
  • Shareholders of record on December 16, 2025, will receive four additional shares for each share held.
  • Distribution of the split shares is scheduled for after market close on or about December 17, 2025.
  • ServiceNow shares are expected to begin trading on a split-adjusted basis on December 18, 2025.

data.title: Hingham Institution for Savings authorizes $20M stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Hingham Institution for Savings (HIFS) announced on Friday that its board of directors has authorized a share repurchase program, pursuant to which the bank may repurchase up to $20 million of its outstanding common stock.
  • Under the Program, repurchases can be made from time to time using a variety of methods, which may include open market purchases, privately negotiated transactions, block trades, or any combination of such methods, in accordance with applicable securities laws and regulations.
  • The Program is authorized through December 6, 2026. The Board anticipates reconsidering the size and terms of the Program on an annual basis.
  • HIFS +1.02% after hours to $304.05.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Commercial Metals upgraded, named top steel pick at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
Commercial Metals (CMC) +3.1% in Friday's trading to a new all-time high $68.35, as J.P. Morgan upgraded shares to Overweight from Neutral with a $78 price target, raised from $64, reflecting strong rebar price support in both the U.S. and E.U., as well as underappreciated value potential from the CVD/AD rebar trade case and pre-cast concrete commercial synergies.

U.S. rebar could remain a bigger beneficiary from displacing imports compared to sheet given
consolidation and mill discipline, JPM analyst Bill Peterson said, adding he also feels stickiness from likely rebar CVD/AD margins is underappreciated amid fluid tariffs, with countries in scope
accounting for ~50% of FY 2024 imports.

In the E.U., Peterson said CBAM and protectionism should offer price support in FY 2026 and beyond, while German stimulus and a potential end to the Ukraine war provide solid call options for incremental demand, and pre-cast concrete also could drive material commercial synergies with existing businesses, which would position the company for bolt-ons given a highly fragmented industry.

The analyst also raised his price target for Overweight-rated Nucor (NUE) to $200 from $175, reflecting a view of lagging valuation, relative utilization upside and strong diversification, both at the mill and downstream levels, which could help protect earnings volatility through the cycle given trade policy uncertainty weighing on underlying steel demand into 2026.

data.title: Consumer credit comes in lower than expected in October
data.description: U.S. consumer credit rose by $9.18B in October, trailing the $11.8B expected and the $11.01B gain in the prior month (revised from $13.09B), according to data released by the Federal Reserve on Friday.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Spotlight on the Fed, Costco earnings, and Nasdaq 100 additions
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - December 8

  • Volatility watch - Bloom Energy (BE) and The Trade Desk (TTD) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Magnitude International (MAGH), Smart Sand (SND), and Haemonetics (HAE), The most oversold stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Columbus Circle Capital Corp. I (BRR), Twin Hospitality Group (TWNP), and Nutanix (NTNX). Traders have their eyes on highly shorted iRobot (IRBT) and Serve Robotics (SERV) amid the flurry of robotics announcements from the White House.
  • IPO watch - Companies expected to price their IPOs and begin trading next week include Cardinal Infrastructure Group (CDNL), Lumexa Imaging Holdings (LMRI), and Wealthfront Corp. (WLTH). The IPO lock-up period expires on certain blocks of shares of Voyager Technologies (VOYG) and AIRO Group (AIRO).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Toll Brothers (TOL).
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Analog Devices (ADI), Albemarle (ALB), UnitedHealth Group (UNH), and Garmin (GRMN).
  • All week - Notable investor conferences during the week include the Barclays Global TMT Conference, Raymond TMT and Consumer Conference, KeyBanc Capital Markets Consumer Conference, ROTH Deer Valley Conference, and Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services Conference. The healthcare sector will have its eyes on the American Society of Hematology Meeting, which will include presentations by Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and Merck (MRK).
  • All day - John Deere (DE) will hold its Analyst Day event. Jefferies expects the company's update on capital discipline and growth optionality to be the key part of the presentation by management.
  • 8:15 a.m. Golden Globe nominations will be announced amid a media landscape being rocked by the announcement that Netflix (NFLX) will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) for $72 billion.
  • 11:00 a.m. The Android Show | XR Edition will be a 30-minute Android XR-focused livestream highlighting all things XR across glasses, headsets, and related form factors, with a heavy emphasis on Gemini-powered experiences. The event is positioned as Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) main showcase for new Android XR platform capabilities following the commercial launch of Samsung's (OTCPK:SSNLF) Galaxy XR headset in the fall.

 

Tuesday - December 9

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Autozone (AZO), Casey's General Stores (CASY), SailPoint (SAIL), and Campbell's Company (CPB). Options trading implies a double-digit move for GameStop (GME) and AeroVironment (AVAV) after they report.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Home Depot's (HD) Investor and Analyst Conference, Graham Holdings' (GHC) Investor Day, The Andersons' (ANDE) Investor Day, CVS Health's (CVS) Investor Day, Elanco's (ELAN) Investor Day, Ligand Pharmaceuticals' (LGND) Investor Day, and an investor update from GE Vernova (GEV).
  • 9:00 a.m. Exxon Mobil (XOM) will hold a webcast for senior leadership to provide an overview of the updated corporate plan.

 

Wednesday - December 10

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Oracle (ORCL), Adobe (ADBE), Chewy (CHWY), and Vail Resorts (MTN). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Planet Labs (PL) and Oxford Industries (OXM) after they report.
  • All day - Shareholders with Electronic Arts (EA) will vote on the $55 billion acquisition offer from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund. The deal is considered the largest leveraged buyout in history.
  • All day - United Natural Foods (UNFI) will hold an Investor Day event.
  • 8:30 a.m. Coinbase Global (COIN) CFO Alesia Haas will participate in a fireside chat at the 53rd Annual Nasdaq London Investor Conference.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will announce its interest rate decision.
  • 2:30 p.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will hold a press conference. Economists will be listening carefully as they assess the Fed's expected interest rate trajectory for 2026.

 

Thursday - December 11

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Broadcom (AVGO), Costco Wholesale (COST), Ciena (CIEN), and Lululemon Athletica (LULU). Options trading implies a double-digit move for RH (RH) and Mitek Systems (MITK) after they report.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Watsco's (WSO) Investor Day, Weyerhaeuser's (WY) Investor Day, Schneider Electric's (OTCPK:SBGSF) Capital Markets Day, Copa Holdings' (COPA) Investor Day, and Suzano's (SUZ) Investor Day.
  • All day - OPEC will publish its monthly oil report.
  • All day - It is the FDA action date on GSK's (GSK) supplemental Gepotidacin treatment.
  • 11:05 a.m. Microsoft (MSFT) will present at the Barclays Global Technology Conference.
  • 5:00 p.m. Costco (COST) will hold its earnings conference call. Investors will be watching for any indications of a special dividend or stock split in the near future. Of interest, Central Garden & Pet (CENT) generates more than 10% of its revenue from Costco and has traded in tandem with it after earnings 88% of the time over the last two years.

 

Friday - December 12

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Johnson Outdoors (JOUT).
  • All day - The FDA action date for BioCryst Pharmaceuticals' (BCRX) Orladeyo (berotralstat) could lead to a share price movement.
  • Postmarket - Nasdaq 100 index changes are expected to be announced based on the schedule used in the past.

 

Senior Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: A tech valuation reset is ‘the big story’ for 2026 – BofA’s Subramanian
data.description:
Savita Subramanian, head of equity and quant strategy at BofA Securities, is warning of a potential valuation reset for mega-cap technology companies (VGT), (IYW), (XLK), (MAGS) despite projecting strong earnings growth.

In an interview with CNBC, Subramanian expressed concern that investors are “buying the dream” with tech stocks, paying peak or near-peak multiples for companies building out power infrastructure and data centers for artificial intelligence without clear evidence of near-term monetization.

“We’re paying a high multiple for growth stocks, but we don’t exactly know how this all plays out over the next few years,” she said, noting that real monetization of AI investments may not materialize until 2026 due to power constraints and implementation challenges.

While Subramanian forecasts robust 14% earnings growth for next year, reaching $310 per share, she believes markets won’t necessarily follow suit.

“The valuation reset is the big story for next year,” she said, adding that “historically, if you look at years of really strong earnings growth, you haven’t necessarily seen the strongest market gains.”

Contrary to market consensus, Subramanian recommends increasing exposure to consumer staples (XLP) and discount retailers (WMT), (DG), (TJX), (ROST)…

She believes the lower-income consumer segment could see improvement in the months ahead due to several factors, including potentially higher tax refunds and government efforts to address inflation ahead of the midterm elections.

data.title: U.S. stocks in the green as NFLX-WBD acquisition fuels rally, investors assess inflation gauge
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were higher on Friday, as traders assessed fresh inflation data in hopes of gauging the Federal Reserve's monetary policy, while news of Netflix ((NFLX)) acquiring Warner Bros. Discovery ((WBD)) dominated news.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.2% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.3%. 

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) -2.4% announced it will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) +5.1%, including its film and television studios, HBO Max and HBO, for a total enterprise value of ~$82.7 billion.

“This creates basically a two-horse show. Disney (DIS), with physical assets and parks, similar to Comcast (CMCSA); and Netflix (NFLX) with tons of streaming and movie assets. Paramount (PSKY) [is] stuck now treading water. [David Ellison, CEO of Paramount Skydance] is the big loser here. Cable assets will all be shed from all. Disney needs to do the same next,” said Ross Gerber, co-founder, CEO and CIO of Gerber Kawasaki.

In economic news, core PCE inflation stalled at +0.2% MoM in September as expected. Meanwhile, December consumer sentiment was higher at 53.3 vs. 52.0 consensus and 51.0 in November, according to the initial UofM Survey.

“Income [is] up 0.4%, spending 0.3%. However, inflation adjusted spending was flat which implied a soft spending path heading into the holiday spending season,” said Joseph Brusuelas, RSM U.S. principal and chief economist.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 5 basis points to 3.58%.

data.title: Tech Voices: NYT vs. Perplexity, SoftBank-DigitalBridge, SpaceX
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • The New York Times (NYT) is suing Perplexity for allegedly copying and distributing its copyrighted content.

“While we believe in the ethical and responsible use and development of AI, we firmly object to Perplexity’s unlicensed use of our content to develop and promote their products,” NYT spokesperson Graham James said in a statement, according to CNBC. “We will continue to work to hold companies accountable that refuse to recognize the value of our work.”

CNBC noted that the NYT is also suing OpenAI (OPENAI) and Microsoft (MSFT) for using its content to train AI models.

  • SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) is reportedly in discussions to buy private equity group DigitalBridge (DBRG), which has been investing in data centers, and take it private.

According to Bloomberg, a deal could be announced in the coming weeks. DigitalBridge's portfolio includes AIMS, AtlasEdge, DataBank, Switch, Vantage Data Centers, and Yondr Group.

  • SpaceX (SPACE) is planning a secondary share sale that values the company at $800 billion, which would make it the most valuable private company in the U.S., according to The Wall Street Journal.

The company had been valued at $400 billion during a secondary share sale in July, the WSJ added.

SpaceX is controlled by Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk.
